en:
Last week I went to the theatre to see a very good play.
I sat down and expected a quiet evening.
A man and a woman behind me talked loudly all the time.
I could not hear the actors, so I turned round angrily.
I said that the play was interesting and I could not bear the noise.
The young man answered rudely that it was none of my business.
cn:
上周我去剧院看了一场很精彩的戏。
我坐下后本来期待一个安静的夜晚。
我身后的一男一女却一直大声说话。
我听不见演员的台词，于是生气地转过身。
我说这出戏很有意思，我实在受不了噪音。
那个年轻人却无礼地回答说这不关我的事。

en:
I always get up late on Sundays because I like resting.
Yesterday it was raining outside and I stayed in bed until noon.
Just then my aunt rang me and asked what I was doing.
I answered that I was having breakfast at that moment.
She repeated my words in surprise because it was already one o'clock.
I laughed and said that Sunday breakfast is often my Sunday lunch.
cn:
我星期天总是起得很晚，因为我喜欢休息。
昨天下着雨，我一直在床上待到中午。
就在那时我姑妈给我打电话，问我在做什么。
我回答说我那时正在吃早饭。
她吃惊地重复了我的话，因为那时已经一点了。
我笑着说周日的早饭常常就是我的周日午饭。

en:
Last summer I went abroad for a holiday by the sea.
On the first day I bought many postcards for my friends.
I spent the whole morning thinking about what to write.
After lunch I sat in a public garden and read my cards again.
A friendly waiter lent me a pen, but still I wrote nothing.
In the end I made a decision and did not send a single card.
cn:
去年夏天我去国外海边度假。
第一天我就给朋友们买了很多明信片。
我花了一整个上午想该写些什么。
午饭后我坐在一个公共花园里，又把卡片读了一遍。
一位友好的服务员借给我一支笔，但我还是一个字也没写。
最后我做了个决定，一张明信片也不寄了。

en:
I have just received a letter from my younger brother in Australia.
He has worked for a large firm there for six months.
He has already visited many different places in that country.
Now he has settled in the centre of a big city.
He has never been abroad before, so everything feels exciting to him.
After reading his letter, I have begun to dream about a long trip too.
cn:
我刚刚收到弟弟从澳大利亚寄来的信。
他已经在那里一家大公司工作了六个月。
他已经去过那个国家许多不同的地方。
现在他已经在一座大城市中心安顿下来。
他以前从未出过国，所以一切都让他觉得兴奋。
读完他的信后，我也开始梦想一次长途旅行。

en:
A businessman had two offices and needed to send urgent messages every day.
The distance from one office to the other was about ten miles.
He trained a pigeon and sent notes in a small cage on its leg.
In this way the bird covered the distance in only three minutes.
The pigeon carried every request safely from one office to the other.
Because of this clever service, he never dialed a wrong number again.
cn:
一位商人有两个办公室，每天都要发送紧急信息。
从一个办公室到另一个大约有十英里。
他训练了一只鸽子，把便条放在它腿上的小笼子里。
就这样，这只鸟只用三分钟就飞完了这段路程。
这只鸽子总能把每一条请求安全送到另一个办公室。
因为这种聪明的服务，他再也不用担心拨错号码了。

en:
I had moved to a new house when a beggar first knocked at my door.
He asked me for a meal and a glass of beer in a polite voice.
After he finished the food, he stood on his head and sang loudly.
I gave him another coin in return for that strange show.
Later my neighbour told me that his name is Percy Buttons.
He calls at every house in our street once a month.
cn:
我刚搬到新家时，一位乞丐第一次来敲门。
他很客气地向我要一顿饭和一杯啤酒。
吃完后，他倒立着大声唱起歌来。
为了这场奇怪的表演，我又给了他一枚硬币。
后来邻居告诉我，他叫珀西·巴顿斯。
他每个月都会来我们这条街的每一户人家一次。

en:
Two detectives were waiting at the airport early in the morning.
They were expecting a plane from South Africa with a valuable parcel.
When the plane landed, two guards took the parcel to the Customs House.
The officers opened it at once because they feared that diamonds were inside.
To everyone's surprise, the parcel was full of stones and sand.
The thieves had stolen the precious diamonds while the detectives were waiting.
cn:
清晨两名侦探正在机场等候。
他们正在等一架从南非飞来的飞机，上面有个贵重包裹。
飞机降落后，两名警卫把包裹送到海关大楼。
官员们立刻打开它，因为他们担心里面有钻石。
让所有人吃惊的是，包裹里全是石头和沙子。
就在侦探们守候时，窃贼已经偷走了珍贵的钻石。

en:
Joe has the most beautiful garden in our town.
His paths are neater, his flowers are better, and his pool is the best.
Bill works very hard too, but his garden is always worse than Joe's.
I enter the competition every year with hope.
Yet my garden is the worst of all because weeds grow everywhere.
Even so, I still enjoy it, because being the worst can be interesting.
cn:
乔拥有我们镇上最漂亮的花园。
他的小路更整洁，花更好，水池也是最棒的。
比尔也很努力，但他的花园总是比乔的差一点。
我每年都满怀希望参加比赛。
可我的花园始终是最差的，因为到处都是杂草。
即便如此，我还是喜欢它，因为当“最差”也挺有趣。

en:
On New Year's Eve a large crowd gathered in the town square.
Everyone looked at the big clock and waited for midnight.
At a quarter to twelve the clock face lit up and the hands moved.
When the minute hand reached twelve, both hands suddenly stopped.
The great clock refused to welcome the new year.
So people laughed, looked at their own watches, and shouted together.
cn:
除夕夜，一大群人聚集在镇中心广场。
大家都看着大钟，等待午夜到来。
十一点四十五分时，钟面亮了，指针也在走。
当分针走到十二点时，两根指针突然停住了。
这座大钟拒绝为新年报时。
于是人们笑着看自己的手表，一起大声欢呼。

en:
Our family has an old piano that was made in Germany.
It was bought by my grandmother many years ago.
Recently it was damaged by a visitor who tried to play jazz.
One of the strings was broken and my father was very angry.
Now the piano has been repaired by a careful worker.
No one is allowed to touch it without my father's permission.
cn:
我们家有一架在德国制造的旧钢琴。
它是很多年前我祖母买下的。
最近一位客人想弹爵士乐，把它弄坏了。
有一根琴弦断了，我父亲非常生气。
现在这架钢琴已经被一位细心的工人修好了。
没有父亲的允许，谁都不准碰它。

en:
I was having dinner when my old friend Tony came into the restaurant.
He used to be a lawyer, but now he works in a bank.
Years ago I had borrowed some money from him and paid it back quickly.
He has never borrowed from me before, but that evening he asked me for money.
I smiled and said that one good turn deserves another.
Then I asked him to pay for my dinner first.
cn:
我正在吃晚饭时，老朋友托尼走进了餐馆。
他过去是律师，但现在在银行工作。
多年前我曾向他借过钱，而且很快就还清了。
他以前从没向我借过钱，但那晚他向我开口了。
我笑着说礼尚往来。
然后我请他先替我把这顿饭钱付了。

en:
Our neighbour Captain Alison will set out tomorrow morning.
He will sail his small boat across the Atlantic in an important race.
The journey will take many months and the sea will test him.
He is proud of his little boat and we are proud of him.
I will go to the harbour early to see him off.
Before he leaves, we will all say goodbye and good luck.
cn:
我们的邻居艾利森船长明早就要出发。
他将驾驶小船横渡大西洋参加一场重要比赛。
这段航程将持续数月，大海会考验他。
他为自己的小船骄傲，我们也为他骄傲。
我会一早去港口给他送行。
在他离开前，我们都会对他说再见并祝他好运。
